human_verification.notes: Reviewed the child-care family-engagement package for authority-source quality, child-care specificity, execution-state and handoff control, closure evidence, refusal posture, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to child-care family-engagement execution, evidence handling, and handoffs across classroom, family-services, subsidy, and community-resource contexts.

Authority sources cover federal child-care and Head Start family-engagement rules, official family-engagement workflow guidance, and child-care health and safety control expectations with refresh and decay metadata.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete inputs, blocked referrals, unsupported completion, ambiguous ownership, unsafe handoffs, custody and subsidy overreach, safety-sensitive escalation, source conflict, tenant adaptation, and systems-of-record conflict.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/child-care-and-family-services/family-engagement-specialist-child-care/ as the canonical NAICS-derived overlay path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around custody, release rights, subsidy decisions, licensing findings, safety escalation, legal interpretation, and clinical or mandated-reporting authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated child-care family-engagement overlay where custody, subsidy, safety, clinical, licensing, and legal authority remain external.
The package is intentionally specification-based for runtime until a separately approved retained implementation is promoted.
